items.raw_summary:
BREAKING: OpenAI has raised $110 billion at a $730 billion valuation, the largest private tech funding round ever.

The backers include Amazon ($50 billion), Softbank ($30 billion) and NVIDIA ($30 billion).

The AI hype isn't slowing down at all. https://t.co/nSjOPt1KyB
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI's $110B raise at $730B valuation — backed by Amazon ($50B), SoftBank ($30B), and NVIDIA ($30B) — is the largest private tech funding round ever and signals massive infrastructure investment ahead.
items.action: Factor AI infrastructure scaling into your roadmap

items.title: Yesterday we reached an agreement with the Department of War for deploying advanced AI systems in cl
items.raw_summary:
Yesterday we reached an agreement with the Department of War for deploying advanced AI systems in classified environments, which we requested they make available to all AI companies.

We think our deployment has more guardrails than any previous agreement for classified AI deploy
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI signs agreement to deploy AI in DoD classified environments — a direct contrast to Anthropic's refusal — marking a pivotal fork in how leading AI labs approach military contracts.
items.action: Understand the diverging military AI strategies across labs

items.title: JUST IN: Perplexity launched "Perplexity Computer" — and it might be the most complete AI agent syst
items.raw_summary:
JUST IN: Perplexity launched "Perplexity Computer" — and it might be the most complete AI agent system available right now.

Not a chatbot upgrade. Not a research tool with a new name.

A system that plans entire projects, delegates to specialist AI models, and runs autonomously for hours, days, or months (their words).

Here's what makes the architecture genuinely different:

→ Opus 4.6 handles core reasoning and orchestration
→ Gemini handles deep research (spawning its own sub-agents)
→ Grok
items.why_it_matters: Perplexity Computer is a full autonomous agent system using Opus 4.6 for planning, with specialist model delegation and multi-day task execution — a serious entrant in the agentic AI space.
items.action: Evaluate Perplexity Computer for long-running autonomous workflows

items.title: Claude is #1 in the App Store today — I want to say a huge thank you to all of our new (and existing
items.raw_summary: Claude is #1 in the App Store today — I want to say a huge thank you to all of our new (and existing!) users for the support. We’re working hard for you, please share your thoughts and feedback along the way. https://t.co/xbCLxqR0Yi

items.title: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory,
items.raw_summary: 4% of GitHub public commits are being authored by Claude Code right now. At the current trajectory, we believe that Claude Code will be 20%+ of all daily commits by the end of 2026. While you blinked, AI consumed all of software development. https://t.co/pFti4r6uR9

items.title: OpenAI fires an employee for prediction market insider trading
items.raw_summary: Prediction markets like Polymarket and Kalshi are big business, and some Big Tech employees are testing boundaries by making trades based on insider knowledge. (HN: 242 pts, 131 comments)

items.title: 🚨 BREAKING: Alibaba just handed the AI agent community a production-grade sandbox for free.
items.raw_summary:
🚨 BREAKING: Alibaba just handed the AI agent community a production-grade sandbox for free.

OpenSandbox is a full-stack platform for running untrusted agent code safely:

→ Unified APIs across multi-language SDKs
→ Docker and Kubernetes runtimes purpose-built for agents
→ Browser automation, VS Code desktop, and network isolation included
→ Designed for coding agents, GUI agents, evaluation, and beyond

Not a side project. Built by Alibaba. Open source.

1.5k stars (+1,100 this week). The secu
items.why_it_matters: Alibaba open-sourced OpenSandbox — a production-grade sandbox for running untrusted agent code with Docker/K8s runtimes, browser automation, and network isolation — solving a critical infrastructure gap for agent builders.
items.action: Evaluate OpenSandbox for your agent execution environment

items.title: 🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read th
items.raw_summary:
🚨 Holy shit… Harvard and Stanford recently released the most unsettling AI agent paper I've read this year.

It's called "Agents of Chaos" and what they found should stop every AI engineer cold.

No theoretical simulations. No cherry-picked benchmarks. A live lab. Real infrastructure. Real failures.

Here's what emerged:

- Agents complied with non-owners who impersonated admins
- Sensitive information leaked across agent boundaries
- One agent executed destructive system-level commands
- Cross
items.why_it_matters: Harvard/Stanford's 'Agents of Chaos' paper tested AI agents in live infrastructure and found they comply with impersonated admins and leak sensitive data — critical findings for anyone deploying agents in production.
items.action: Read the paper and audit your agent access controls

items.title: 28 Days of Claude API - Day 28 - The Agent SDK
items.raw_summary:
28 Days of Claude API - Day 28 - The Agent SDK

The raw API gives you Claude. The Agent SDK gives you Claude with hands.

File access. Bash. Web search. Subagents. Sessions. MCP. All built in. You define the task and the boundaries and Claude handles the rest.
items.why_it_matters: The Claude Agent SDK bundles file access, bash, web search, subagents, sessions, and MCP into a single framework — giving developers a production-ready toolkit for building Claude-powered agents.
items.action: Try the Agent SDK for your next agent project

items.title: A Chinese official’s use of ChatGPT revealed an intimidation operation
items.raw_summary: A sprawling Chinese influence operation — accidentally revealed by a Chinese law enforcement official’s use of ChatGPT — focused on intimidating Chinese dissidents abroad, including by impersonating US immigration officials, according to a new report from ChatGPT-maker OpenAI. (HN: 65 pts, 45 comments)
items.why_it_matters: OpenAI uncovered a Chinese influence operation using ChatGPT to intimidate dissidents abroad by impersonating US immigration officials — demonstrating both AI misuse risks and detection capabilities.
items.action: Review OpenAI's threat intelligence report

items.why_it_matters: llama.cpp b8184 adds Vulkan partial offloading improvements for AMD GPUs with async transfers, making local LLM inference significantly faster on non-NVIDIA hardware.
items.action: Update llama.cpp if running on AMD GPUs

items.title: Microgpt explained interactively
items.raw_summary: Walk through Karpathy's 200-line GPT from scratch. Tokenize names into integers, watch softmax convert scores to probabilities, step through backpropagation on a computation graph, explore attention heatmaps, and see a tiny model learn to generate plausible names. (HN: 125 pts, 8 comments)
items.why_it_matters: An interactive walkthrough of Karpathy's 200-line GPT lets you visualize tokenization, softmax, backpropagation, and attention heatmaps step by step — the best hands-on learning resource for understanding transformers.
items.action: Work through the interactive tutorial at growingswe.com
